narrative: OMX30 faces a conflicted morning as Continental weakness, UK political turmoil, and sharper US yields clash with a structurally bullish 200-MA backdrop. The weighted global correlation signal is mildly negative at -0.332%, with Europe down 0.5–1.2% but US and Asia holding steady. Domestically, the technicals lack urgency: MACD is below signal (hist -5.041), RSI sits neutral at 40.8, and ADX 21.9 signals moderate but bearish directional bias—below the confidence threshold of 18. Oil's 3.5% surge and USD/SEK strength (+38bp) help exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson) but simultaneously lift borrowing costs and input inflation, pressuring consumer and cyclical stocks. The OMX30 is +7.1% above the 200-MA and only +0.5% above the 50-MA, leaving it vulnerable to profit-taking if momentum stalls.
morningBrief: OMX30 opens into headwinds: European equities sink on political and macro uncertainty (Starmer crisis rattles UK gilt markets, DAX/CAC weakness spreads), while yields spike 105bp in US 10Y (now 4.41%). This is a classic cross-current—banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) gain NIM tailwind, but real estate (Balder, Castellum) and tech (Ericsson, Hexagon) face DCF pressure. Brent crude jumps 3.5% to 107.8, a double-edged sword: help for energy input cost pass-through, but headwind for consumer discretionary and refining margins. USD/SEK at 9.278 (+38bp) aids large-cap exporters, but watch for mean reversion in OMX30: at +0.5% vs 50-MA and with MACD below signal, any break below 3085 (lower Bollinger) could trigger stops. VIX up 239bp to 18.82—risk-off undertone. Watch US 14:30 CET Jobless Claims; any surprise could amplify volatility. Copper +1.03% benefits Boliden (mining positive). Play banks on yield support, trim cyclical exposure into resistance at 3180.
drivers: Continental Europe weakness (DAX -1.17%, CAC -0.64%) offsetting modest US resilience
Yield spike (US 10Y +105bp) creates cross-current: banks benefit, tech/RE pressured
Oil rally (+3.49% Brent) damps consumer/industrials margin outlook despite stronger USD/SEK exporters benefit
OMX30 technically mixed: long-term bull structure (+7.1% vs 200-MA) but MACD bearish below signal, RSI neutral at 40.8, ADX moderate (21.9) reducing conviction

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield surge to 4.41% (+105bp) widens net interest margin expectations and deposit beta compression favors net interest income growth for Swedish systemically important banks.
Sharp yield rally increases refinancing costs and compresses valuation multiples on leveraged property portfolios; DCF-sensitive asset class.
Yield spike compresses DCF valuations; NASDAQ held flat overnight but European sentiment sours on macro headwinds; growth-weighted equities under pressure.

USD/SEK strength (+38bp to 9.278) supports export competitiveness and translational earnings, but Brent oil +3.49% increases input costs and offsets margin expansion. Volvo B benefits from strong USD; Sandvik faces steel/energy cost headwinds.
Copper +1.03% and gold holding firm (down only 0.14%) provide direct revenue tailwinds for Boliden. SSAB pressured by higher energy costs, offset partially by oil-driven commodity premium.
Brent oil +3.49% increases transport and energy costs; consumer discretionary vulnerable in risk-off environment. Weak EUR/SEK (EUR/SEK +16bp to 10.89) modestly pressures H&M sourcing costs.
Defensive rotation into risk-off conditions (VIX +239bp); AstraZeneca and Essity benefit from haven-bid and lower beta profile amid macro uncertainty.

watchList.reason: Copper +1.03% and gold holding flat; direct commodity exposure with strong upside if oil-driven energy inflation narrative sustains. Clear beneficiary of current risk repricing.
watchList.catalyst: Copper and gold prices; any further commodity outperformance

watchList.reason: Yield spike (US 10Y +105bp) is potent NIM driver. Bank sector typically lags initial spike but reprices higher once duration shock settles. 18% of OMX30 weighting.
watchList.catalyst: 10Y yield stability; any further rate curve steepening

watchList.reason: USD/SEK strength (+38bp) helps export translation; Industrials at 28% of index. Watch for breakout if OMX30 holds 3123 pivot. Brent headwind partially offset by strong currency tailwind.
watchList.catalyst: USD/SEK durability; global industrial PMI sentiment

watchList.reason: Yield spike compression + weak European sentiment + NASDAQ flat overnight = perfect storm for growth-weighted telecom. Tech sector under pressure.
watchList.catalyst: Any further yield rise or tech selloff; 5G demand signals

watchList.reason: Consumer discretionary weakness (oil up pressures spend, consumer confidence at risk), weak EUR/SEK headwind on sourcing, and risk-off tone all negative. Most vulnerable in current regime.
Defensive name gaining haven demand in risk-off environment. Flight-to-quality bid into macro uncertainty. Pharma weighting 12%; AZ is largest single defensive bet.
watchList.catalyst: VIX stabilization; any further macro deterioration favours defensives

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET (Tuesday 12 May) — watch for any upside surprise in initial claims as potential risk-off accelerant. No major Swedish economic releases today. ECB speakers (Lagarde attending Council) later in week will be key for EUR/SEK and rate expectations.
topRisk: Further escalation in UK political crisis could trigger gilts selloff and broader risk-off cascade into equities; DAX weakness (-1.17%) suggests Continental contagion risk. Oil spike to 107.8 (now at multi-month high) could stall if demand fears resurface. Any MACD breakdown below signal combined with breach of 3085 Bollinger lower could trigger momentum unwind given RSI neutrality and ADX moderation.
technicalSummary: Medium-term warning: MACD below signal (hist -5.041), bearish crossover risk
RSI 40.8 neutral (no extreme reading, no mean reversion extremes)
ADX 21.9 moderate with bearish DI bias (below confidence threshold of 25; reduce conviction 30%)
Bollinger %B=0.28 (price in lower half, more room to move down to support)
ROC-90 strong +7.0% (momentum still positive but weakening given MACD signal)
52-week 80% range: -4.9% from high (some cushion but not excessive)
